L1.area: Query parameter binding and serialization layer

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists in the JDBC driver where parameter values are interpolated directly into SQL query strings without adequate sanitization when using a non-default query mode. A negated parameter value can produce a line comment sequence that comments out the remainder of the query, enabling arbitrary SQL injection.

L3.area: Query parameter serialization in the simple (non-extended) query mode within the core protocol layer
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists in the JDBC driver's parameter serialization logic when the non-default simple query mode is used. When a numeric parameter value is negated in the SQL query, the serialization can produce a double-dash line comment sequence that comments out the remainder of the SQL statement, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL commands via crafted parameter values.

ground_truth.reason: The SimpleParameterList class serializes query parameters into SQL text without properly escaping or quoting the values when using simple query mode, allowing SQL injection via crafted parameter values that exploit line comment generation through negation.
